briefs.title: Burnaby to Replace Zoning Bylaw 1965 with Updated Bylaw 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby plans to replace its current zoning bylaw from 1965 with a new one, Bylaw 2026. This includes updated guidelines for development permits and changes to zoning districts. The goal is to modernize zoning rules in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 and related documents.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL
AND REPLACEMENT
[image]
1.Burnaby Zoning Bylaw - Proposed Repeal and Replacement
2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026
2026-04-28_.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Proposed Form and Character Development
Permit Area Guidelines 2026-04-28.pdf
4.Attachment 3 - Proposed Streamside Protection and
Enhancement Development Permit Area Guidelines
2026-04-28.pdf
5.Attachment 4 - Proposed Tenant Protection Development
Permit Area Guidelines 2026-04-28.pdf
6.Attachment 5 - Proposed Zone Reassignment Reference Maps
2026-04-28.pdf
7.Attachment 6 - Rental Replacement and Inclusionary
Requirements Evaluation 2026-04-28.pdf
8.Attachment 7 - Example Inclusionary and Rental
Replacement at Occupancy and Second Tenancy
2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning
Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning
Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area
Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority Asks for More Operating Funds
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) has asked the City of Burnaby for money to help run its programs in Year 2. The BHA needs the City Council to approve this funding request.
briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2
OPERATING FUNDING
[image]
1.BHA 2026 Operating Funding 2026-04-28_.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - BHA Assistance Request for Year 2 Operating
Funds 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing
Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating
Funding.

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves 2026 Property Tax Rates for Municipal, Regional, and BIA
briefs.summary: Burnaby has approved the 2026 property tax rates. These rates cover municipal services, regional services, and Business Improvement Areas (BIAs). BIAs are specific areas where businesses work together to improve their local business environment.
briefs.action_required: Review the 2026 property tax rates to understand your business's financial obligations.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
[image]
1.2026 Property Tax rates 2026-04-28__.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026_2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal,
regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax
rates.

briefs.title: New Approving Officer Appointed
briefs.summary: A new Approving Officer has been appointed. The Approving Officer is responsible for tasks such as approving subdivision plans (dividing land into smaller parcels).
briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
[image]
1.Appointment of Approving Officer 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving
Officer.

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Variance for Retaining Wall Height at 8012 11th Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01. This permit allows a change to the rules about how high retaining walls can be at 8012 11th Avenue. The permit lets the property owner build retaining walls that are higher than normally allowed.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING
WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
[image]
1.DVP26-01 - 8012 11th Avenue 2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - DVP 26-01 - Sketch1 and 2 2026-04-28.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - DVP 26-01 - Permit 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance
Permit (DVP) #26-01.

briefs.title: Property and Liability Insurance Contracts Approved for 2026
briefs.summary: The city has approved the renewal of property and liability insurance contracts for 2026. This covers insurance programs for city-owned property and potential liability claims.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT
RENEWALS

[image]
1.2026 Insurance Program - Contract Renewals -
2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for
the annual property and liability insurance programs.

briefs.title: Networking Software Licenses Contract Awarded
briefs.summary: A contract has been awarded for networking software licenses. This ensures the continued supply and delivery of these licenses.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
[image]
1.CA - Networking Software Licenses 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

briefs.title: New Civic Facility Contract Awarded for Fit-Out, Furnishings, and Equipment
briefs.summary: A contract has been awarded to supply, deliver, and install office and facility furniture for a new civic building. This ensures the facility is properly equipped for its intended use.
briefs.source_body:

CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS
AND EQUIPMENT
[image]
1.CA - New Civic Facility Fit-Out 2026-04-28 ___.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility
furniture.

briefs.title: External Audit Services Contract Change Approved
briefs.summary: A contract change for external audit services has been approved. This means the city has agreed to modify the existing agreement with the company that checks the city's financial records. The details of the specific changes are in the attached documents.
briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
[image]
1.CC - External Audit Services 2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (External Audit Services)
2026-04-28 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change
(CC) for External Audit Services.

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex geotechnical consulting contract change approved
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a change to the contract for geotechnical (soil and rock) consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project. This change likely involves adjustments to the scope, timeline, or cost of the existing contract with the consulting firm.
briefs.source_body:

GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
[image]
1.CC - BLRC Geotechnical Consulting Services 2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (BLRC Geotechnical
Consulting Services) 2026-04-28 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change
(CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake
Recreation Complex project.

briefs.title: Spring 2026 Community Grant Recipients Approved
briefs.summary: The city council has approved the list of organizations that will receive Community Grants for the Spring 2026 intake. These grants support various community initiatives.

briefs.title: Burnaby Fees & Charges Bylaw: Some Fees Change June & September 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby is changing some fees in its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. Some fees change on June 1, 2026. Other fees change on September 1, 2026. Check the updated bylaw to see if these changes affect your business.
briefs.action_required: Check the updated bylaw to see if your fees are changing.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fees and charges bylaw changes take effect
Burnaby fees and charges bylaw changes take effect

briefs.source_body:

briefs.title: Burnaby Endorses New Tenant Protection Bylaw
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby is moving forward with a new Tenant Protection Bylaw. This bylaw aims to protect renters in Burnaby. It includes a framework for tenant protection and associated bylaw changes.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed bylaw to understand your obligations as a landlord.
briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION
FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW
AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
[image]
1.PDC - Proposed Tenant Protection Framework
2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Proposed Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw
2026 2026-04-28.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Key Terms and Changes between TAP and the
proposed Burnaby TPB 2026-04-28.pdf
4.Attachment 3 - Proposed Amendments to Burnaby Bylaw
Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2026-04-28.pdf
5.Attachment 4 - List of in-progress Rezoning Applications
subject to TAP 2026-04-28.pdf

Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant
protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw
and associated bylaw amendments.

briefs.title: Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project Improvements Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved the proposed improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. This project aims to improve the bikeway for better accessibility and safety.

8.4

PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS

4.Attachment 3 - Big Bend Area Proposed Cross-Sections

briefs.title: Burnaby to change intersection design standards for safety.
briefs.summary: Burnaby is changing its Town Centre Design Standards. The change will include protected intersection geometry. This will improve road safety for people walking and cycling. It also supports mobility for all ages and abilities.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. Stay informed about changes in Burnaby's design standards.
briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY
TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO
INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION
GEOMETRY
[image]
1.TC - Proposed Amendment to the Burnaby Town Centre
Design Standards 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby
Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected
intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and
cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers Exempting Some Properties from Density Requirements
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is considering whether to exempt some properties in the Brentwood Park area from new provincial rules that allow for more density (more housing units in an area). Some properties have restrictive covenants (agreements that limit how the land can be used). The council will decide whether these covenants should override the new provincial density rules.
briefs.action_required: Monitor City of Burnaby updates on this issue.
briefs.source_body:
9.1
BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION -
RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY
BUILDING SCHEMES
[image]
1.Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and
Statutory Building Schemes 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion
regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with
Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.

briefs.title: Burnaby Building Permits: Construction Activity Report Published
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby published a report on building permit activity for March 2026. The report gives the City Council information on construction projects based on the building permits issued during that time.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03
FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
[image]
1.SU - Building Permit Activity Report No. 03 2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Building Permit Activity Report No. 03
2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction
activity as reflected by the building permits that have been
issued for the subject period.

briefs.title: Burnaby rezoning bylaw amended for Grand Promenade: Hotel, restaurant uses added.
briefs.summary: Burnaby has amended the zoning bylaw for the Grand Promenade area. The changes allow for replacing some commercial office space with hotel and restaurant uses. The amendment also increases the number of market strata (condominium) units by 362, adding seven and four storeys to two towers respectively. Also, the inclusionary housing requirement will be transferred to the Carrigan site.
briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service, Construction & Development
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950
Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
[image]
1.14808 Amend No. 7, 2026 REZ 24-23 (Grand
Promenade_Austin Rd_Gatineau PL).ACD 2026.pdf

Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands
pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received
Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two
market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73
(Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail
and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning
application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of
the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and
add 362 additional market strata units from the development
concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional
seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In
addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning
application, the applicant is proposing to provide the
inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be
transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from
the previous rezoning approval.
(Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby rezoning approved for Kingsway mixed-use development
briefs.summary: Burnaby approved Amendment Bylaw #14809 to allow a 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway. The building will have 290 apartments, commercial space at street level, and underground parking. This change aligns with the Official Community Plan and the Edmonds Community Plan.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
[image]
1.14809 Amendment No. 8, 2026 REZ 25-09 (6535
Kingsway).CD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use
multiple-family development with a double-height commercial
storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units,
ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and
underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the
applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations
for the site.
(Item 8.3., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Zoning Change for Self-Storage Building on Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby has approved a change to its zoning rules to allow a five-story self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. This change follows the city's official community plan (OCP) policies from 1998 and transitional rules.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
[image]
1.14810 Amendment No. 9, 2026 REZ 25-21 (2757 Norland
Ave).CD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed
self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in
accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and
approved transitionary provisions.
(Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby E-Bike Pilot Program: New Rules for Electric Bikes and Scooters
briefs.summary: Burnaby is updating its traffic laws to include a pilot program for e-bikes (electric bikes) and electric kick scooters. The changes will affect where and how these vehicles can be used on city streets.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw for e-bike and scooter rules.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961,
Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
[image]
1.14811.Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961 Amendment
Bylaw No. 1 2026 (E-Bike and Electric Pilot Program).pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-
bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby Parks: E-bikes and Electric Kick Scooters Allowed Under Pilot Program
briefs.summary: Burnaby has amended its Parks Regulation Bylaw to allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in some parks as part of a pilot program. This means people can now use these devices in certain designated park areas.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby park signage for e-bike and scooter-approved areas.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979,
Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026

[image]
1.14812. Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amend Bylaw
No. 1 2026 (E-Bike and Electric Pilot Program).pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979
(e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby Parks: E-bike, Scooter Rules to be Enforced
briefs.summary: Burnaby has updated its rules for dealing with violations of e-bike and electric kick scooter rules in parks. The city can now issue tickets for breaking these rules. This change is part of a pilot program to manage e-bikes and scooters in Burnaby parks.
briefs.action_required: Review Burnaby's e-bike and scooter rules for parks to avoid fines.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009,
Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick
scooter programs)
[image]
1.14813 Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amend Bylaw
No. 3, 2026 BBY Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 .pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement
Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby Sets 2026 Property Tax Rates
briefs.summary: Burnaby has set the property tax rates for 2026. This bylaw decides how much tax you will pay on your land and any buildings on it. These rates are used to fund city services.
briefs.action_required: Review your 2026 property tax bill for accuracy.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Construction & Development, Grocery & Food Retail, Liquor, Cannabis, Healthcare, Finance & Insurance, Technology, Natural Resources, Transportation & Logistics, Agriculture, Labour & Employment, Professional Services, Spa & Wellness
briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
[image]
1.14814.Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 v2.pdf
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and
improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the
Community Charter
(Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council April 28, 2026)

Subject to approval of item 7.3.
